subject: English
title: In the Kingdom of Fools – Hard Short Notes

key_points: The story is a satirical folktale that criticises irrational leadership
The king’s strange laws create disorder and confusion in society
The court scene exposes the absurdity of the justice system
The disciple’s greed puts him in danger
The guru’s intelligence ultimately restores order in the kingdom
characters: The Minister – Symbol of blind loyalty and poor judgement
The Guru – Embodiment of wisdom and strategic thinking
The Disciple – Symbol of human greed and shortsightedness
The People – Represent citizens who obey authority without questioning it
themes: Greed and blind obedience can lead to disaster
literary_devices: Satire – The story ridicules foolish rulers
Irony – The king becomes the victim of his own decision
Symbolism – Restoring the natural day–night cycle symbolises the return of order
exam_tips: Analyse the story as satire on bad governance
Explain the symbolism of the restored day–night cycle
Discuss how humour strengthens the message
Use examples from the court scene to explain the theme
